name: Community Commerce Bank
description_short: Community Commerce Bank is a California-based community bank formed in 1976 offering commercial lending, SBA loans, and deposit accounts through three Southern California branches.
description_long:
Community Commerce Bank (CCB) was established in 1976 and operates as part of The East Los Angeles Community Union (TELACU) family of companies. The bank is headquartered in Claremont, California, and maintains a regional focus on serving California residents through a network of three walk-in branches located in Los Angeles County.

CCB specializes in commercial lending with particular expertise in SBA 7(A) loans and property-based financing. The bank positions itself as a direct lender for small business owners, investors, and brokers seeking commercial property financing within California. Beyond lending, CCB offers high-interest savings accounts and other deposit products designed to serve both individual and commercial customers.

The bank distinguishes itself through its community-oriented approach and specialized focus on commercial real estate lending. As a TELACU-affiliated institution, CCB emphasizes service to underserved California communities and provides personalized guidance from commercial lending experts rather than relying on automated loan processes.

CCB is best suited for commercial borrowers and small business owners seeking SBA or commercial property loans in California, particularly those preferring relationship-based banking with local branch access. However, the bank's limited geographic footprint (three branches in LA County) and apparent focus on commercial rather than consumer lending may restrict accessibility for those seeking traditional personal banking services or living outside Southern California. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: SBA 7(A) loans for small business financing
Commercial property loans for multiple property types
In-person banking at three Southern California branch locations
pros: Direct SBA 7(A) lender with specialized expertise in small business financing
Commercial real estate lending for multiple property types
Three physical branch locations in Los Angeles County for in-person service
Established 48-year track record (founded 1976) as a California-based bank
Part of TELACU family, indicating community-focused mission
Dedicated commercial lending team providing personalized guidance
cons: Limited geographic reach with only three branches, all in Los Angeles County
Website lacks specific information about interest rates, loan terms, or eligibility requirements
No mention of consumer personal loans, mortgages, or retail banking products beyond savings accounts
Email communications noted as unencrypted, creating security concerns for sensitive information
Customer service available only during business hours (9am-5pm PT, M-F)
best_for: Small business owners and entrepreneurs seeking SBA 7(A) financing in California
Commercial real estate investors and brokers in Southern California
California residents interested in community-focused banking with local branch relationships
diagnosis: Community Commerce Bank is best for commercial borrowers and small business owners in Southern California seeking SBA or commercial real estate loans through a community-focused local lender. The main caveat is its limited geographic footprint (three LA County branches) and apparent lack of consumer-focused products, making it unsuitable for those seeking traditional personal banking, mortgages, or services outside Southern California.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
meta_description: Community Commerce Bank is a California-based community bank offering commercial lending, SBA loans, and deposit accounts.
